text:
The behaviour was reportedly the subject of a complaint from a Brazilian journalist, who passed the information onto organisers. He alleged the male appeared to be watching himself on the big screens before making the gesture.

He first made it when Australian Chloe Covell was in action. Minutes later, he repeated the gesture when he was stationed close to Japanese athlete Liz Akama.

The controversy cameamid a gripping final, with Coco Yoshizawa of Japan producing a thrilling display to take gold. Compatriot Liz Akama earned silver, and Rayssa Leal of Brazil bronze.

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

The 14-year-old led the field during qualifying with a combined score of 258.92. And she cemented glory in the final by pulling off a bigspin flip with a boardslide to send the crowd into raptures.

title: Olympics official's accreditation revoked for 'white supremacy gesture' | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Fraser Watson
description: There has been more controversy at the summer Games.
